original_hypothesis.title: Market De-Whitelisting Permanently Freezes User Unclaimed Rewards with No Recovery Path
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When governance calls `whiteListLendingMarket(_market, false)`, the `update_market` require check `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market])` blocks all subsequent calls to `claim()` and `sync_ledger()` for that market. Users with accrued but unclaimed CANTO rewards permanently lose access to those rewards. There is no emergency claim path, no grace period, and no migration mechanism. Re-whitelisting resets `lastRewardBlock` to current block, creating a gap in reward coverage.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a dead-end state: once de-whitelisted, users with pending rewards have no path to claim them. The code provides no emergency withdrawal or admin-triggered distribution mechanism. Even governance cannot recover the situation without contract changes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market], "Market not whitelisted")` in update_market blocks all claim/sync operations; no alternative claim path exists when whitelist is false

preconditions: Users have unclaimed CANTO rewards (rewardDebt < accumulatedCanto)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: De-whitelisting immediately and irreversibly blocks all user claims
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Governance action triggers the freeze — no user recourse

operational_questions: Under what circumstances would governance de-whitelist a market?
How much CANTO could be locked per market at de-whitelisting time?
Could governance pre-call update_market before de-whitelisting to minimize impact?
validation_requirements: Confirm that claim() reverts after de-whitelisting
Test re-whitelisting behavior and its effect on pending rewards
Verify no alternative claim mechanism exists

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: N/A — requires governance action (trusted per docs)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Governance de-whitelists market for any operational reason
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All users with pending rewards lose access permanently
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: CANTO remains locked in contract with no extraction path

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent loss of user rewards for de-whitelisted market
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No recovery mechanism exists in the contract
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All users of the de-whitelisted market

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Any time governance decides to de-whitelist

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
